description:
## Parent

Parent epic: `bsm-dbh` — Filtering and search issue list views.

## Background

The left sidebar already advertises Views and lifecycle choices, but the controls are disabled and static. This feature turns that chrome into the user's primary Issue List View selector. The sidebar lives in the app shell while the Issue List, search box, and Issue Detail live in the Issue Explorer, so this task establishes the shared app-level view model and the count/activation behavior future slices will render through.

An Issue List View is a selectable lens, not a combinable filter. Only one sidebar item is active at a time across both groups, while Issue membership across views may overlap because All, Ready, Blocked, and status views answer different Beadwork questions.

## What to build

Add app-level Issue List View selection and sidebar counts after the combined load succeeds. The available views are exactly:

- All
- Ready
- Blocked
- Open
- In Progress
- Closed
- Deferred

All should remain the default active Issue List View. Clicking a non-active loaded sidebar item changes the active view. Clicking the active item again is a no-op. Sidebar buttons are unavailable during loading and failure and become normal enabled buttons after successful load. Counts are hidden during loading/failure and shown after successful load, including zero counts.

## Technical Approach

Introduce a typed Issue List View ID model that distinguishes stored Beadwork status values from display labels. Use `in_progress` as the internal status view ID and "In Progress" as the display label. Rename the sidebar group currently labeled "States" to "Status" to match Beadwork lifecycle terminology.

App should own the active Issue List View because App owns the sidebar and its counts. App should also own or receive the loaded collections needed to calculate base counts. Counts must be base counts before search is applied:

- All count = `allIssues.length`.
- Ready count = `readyIssues.length`.
- Blocked count = `blockedIssues.length`.
- Status counts = number of All Issues whose stored `status` equals `open`, `in_progress`, `closed`, or `deferred`.

Pass the active view and view-change handler through the App/Issue Explorer integration boundary, without adding a new active-view header near the search bar.

## Considerations

- Counts should not be placeholder values during loading or failure; hide them until data exists.
- Zero counts must render as `0` and remain clickable after load.
- Sidebar controls should be real buttons with normal Tab order and Enter/Space activation when enabled.
- Do not implement combined/faceted filtering. A status view is a view, not a secondary filter layered onto Ready or Blocked.
- Do not persist the active view across app reloads; default to All every app start.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All is the default active Issue List View after successful load.
- [ ] Exactly one sidebar item across Views and Status has the active/current state at a time.
- [ ] Clicking an inactive loaded sidebar item changes the active view; clicking the active item again leaves state unchanged.
- [ ] Sidebar buttons are disabled/unavailable during loading and failure and enabled after successful load.
- [ ] Sidebar counts are hidden during loading/failure and shown after successful load, including `0`.
- [ ] Sidebar counts are base counts and do not depend on the search query implemented in later slices.
- [ ] The group label says `Status`, not `States`.
- [ ] The internal In Progress view uses `in_progress`; the UI label says `In Progress`.
- [ ] No active-view header or label is added near the search box.

## Test Plan

- Component tests for loading/failure disabled sidebar controls and hidden counts.
- Component tests for enabled loaded buttons, one active item, active no-op click behavior, and keyboard-accessible button semantics.
- Component tests for base count values, including zero-count views.
- Component tests or integration tests verifying counts do not change when a search query is later typed.
title: Model Issue List Views and sidebar counts in the app shell
